input.multi_file_hint: For polyploids: separate multiple files with commas   e.g.  subA.gff,subB.gff

basic_options.step_tooltip: Numeric increment between consecutive gene IDs (default: 10).
basic_options.longest_tooltip: Retain only the longest transcript (representative isoform) per locus. Highly recommended for phylogenetic tree construction and Ka/Ks evolutionary rate calculations.
basic_options.keep_tooltip: Skip gzip compression of input files after processing.
basic_options.save_log_tooltip: Write a timestamped  <prefix>_run.log  file in the output directory.

advanced_options.add_prefix_tooltip: Assign isolated ID prefixes to each polyploid subgenome or phased assembly to prevent fatal sequence/ID collisions during fusion.
advanced_options.no_repair_tooltip: Do not infer CDS from exons (or vice versa). Use this when the input contains non-coding genes (lncRNA, tRNA, pseudogenes) to prevent false CDS generation.
advanced_options.pep_qc_tooltip: Report counts of non-ATG starts, internal stop codons, and truncated CDS at run completion. All sequences are still written to output.
advanced_options.bed6_tooltip: Output 6-column BED format (chrom/start/end/name/score/strand) for full compatibility with bedtools and genome browsers. Default is extended 7-column with original ID in col 7.
advanced_options.keep_source_tooltip: Retain the original annotation source tag (e.g. augustus, maker, SNAP) in GFF3 column 2 for provenance tracking.
advanced_options.low_mem_tooltip: Use on-disk byte-offset FASTA indexing instead of loading sequences into RAM. Recommended for very large genomes (e.g. wheat ≥ 15 GB, sugarcane). Requires UNCOMPRESSED FASTA input.

log.idle: Idle — configure parameters in the Run tab and press  ▶ Run Locally

job_script.intro: Fill in the cluster parameters below. The generated script wraps the exact gstd CLI command derived from your Run-tab settings.
job_script.generated_hint: Script generated — review, then copy or save to disk.

file_browser.title: Select File
file_browser.hint: Navigate with arrow keys, Enter to expand, Space to select.

help.title: Help & Quick Reference
help.body:
gstd (Genome Standardizer) v4.1.0

Core workflow:
  1. Set GFF/FASTA paths and an output prefix in the Run tab.
  2. Toggle options as needed.
  3. Click ▶ Run Locally to execute in-terminal, or ⚙ Generate HPC Script for cluster submission.

Polyploid mode:
  Separate multiple GFF/FASTA files with commas.
  Use Subgenome Prefixes to prevent gene ID collisions.

Low-memory mode:
  For genomes > 10 GB, enable Low Memory Mode.
  Input FASTA must be UNCOMPRESSED.

Outputs per species:
  ALL.standard.gff3  |  PREFIX.cds  |  PREFIX.pep
  PREFIX.bed         |  PREFIX.id_list

Keyboard shortcuts:
  Ctrl+Q  Quit          Ctrl+L  Switch Language
  Ctrl+R  Run           Ctrl+S  Generate Script
  F1      This help

validation.count_mismatch: Number of GFF files must equal number of FASTA files.
validation.prefix_count_mismatch: Number of subgenome prefixes must equal number of input file pairs.
validation.step_invalid: Gene ID step must be a positive integer.
validation.walltime_invalid: Walltime must be in HH:MM:SS format.
validation.mem_invalid: Memory must be a positive integer (GB).
validation.nodes_invalid: Node count must be a positive integer.
validation.cpus_invalid: CPU count must be a positive integer.
